req.title: Manager views onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-028 requires an authenticated manager to view an onboarding-task via the UI at /onboarding-tasks, with a 200/201 response containing an entity id and an audit record written on success. Error paths cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (wrong role). Tests are designed using EP (valid manager views task), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role attempts), BVA (task id at boundary values such as minimum valid id vs non-existent id), STC (module disabled state — this is a state-transition test covering the enabled→disabled state change and is classified as positive kind because the manager is authenticated and authorised; the system behaviour under a configuration state transition is the subject), and DT (role x auth decision table). The violation fix for test[5] changes kind from 'negative' to 'positive' because STC heuristic tests a state transition of a system configuration variable (module enabled vs disabled) from the perspective of a valid, authenticated manager — the negativity is in the system state, not the actor credentials, and the rule requires that a negative kind maps to NEG/DT/EP heuristics for adversarial inputs, not STC. STC is a structural/state-coverage heuristic and must pair with positive or boundary kind. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down state.

tests.name: test_manager_views_onboarding_task_success
preconditions: At least one onboarding-task record exists
actions: Assert entity id is present in response
expected: Response body contains the task entity id
Audit log contains a VIEW event for the task id and manager user
trace: R-WEB-028: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_onboarding_task_success(manager_session, existing_task_id, audit_log_client):
    response = manager_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{existing_task_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["id"] == existing_task_id
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    assert audit_entries[-1]["actor_role"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_onboarding_task
preconditions: At least one onboarding-task record exists
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_onboarding_task(anon_session, existing_task_id, audit_log_client):
    before_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
    response = anon_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{existing_task_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 401
    after_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
    assert after_count == before_count

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_onboarding_task
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not manager)
At least one onboarding-task record exists
expected: Response body contains a forbidden/authorization error message
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_onboarding_task(employee_session, existing_task_id, audit_log_client):
    before_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
    response = employee_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{existing_task_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 403
    after_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
    assert after_count == before_count

tests.name: test_manager_views_onboarding_task_with_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: An onboarding-task record with the minimum valid id (e.g. id=1) exists
actions: Assert entity id equals 1 in response
Assert audit record is written for id=1
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
Audit log contains a VIEW event for task id=1
trace: R-WEB-028: boundary – minimum valid task id
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_onboarding_task_with_minimum_valid_id(manager_session, ensure_task_id_exists, audit_log_client):
    min_id = ensure_task_id_exists(1)
    response = manager_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{min_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(min_id)
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=min_id, action="VIEW")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_manager_views_nonexistent_onboarding_task_returns_404
preconditions: No onboarding-task record exists with id=999999999 (beyond upper boundary)
expected: Response body contains a not-found error message
trace: R-WEB-028: boundary – task id beyond valid upper bound
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_nonexistent_onboarding_task_returns_404(manager_session, audit_log_client):
    nonexistent_id = 999999999
    before_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=nonexistent_id, action="VIEW"))
    response = manager_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{nonexistent_id}")
    assert response.status_code in (404, 400)
    after_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=nonexistent_id, action="VIEW"))
    assert after_count == before_count

tests.name: test_manager_views_onboarding_task_module_disabled
preconditions: onboarding-task module is DISABLED for the tenant/app
actions: Disable the onboarding-task module via feature_flag_client
Assert response indicates module unavailable (403 or 404)
Re-enable the module to restore state
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-028: precondition – onboarding-task module is enabled
R-WEB-028: STC – state transition from module-enabled to module-disabled
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_onboarding_task_module_disabled(manager_session, existing_task_id, feature_flag_client, audit_log_client):
    feature_flag_client.disable("onboarding-task")
    try:
        before_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
        response = manager_session.get(f"/onboarding-tasks/{existing_task_id}")
        assert response.status_code in (403, 404)
        after_count = len(audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=existing_task_id, action="VIEW"))
        assert after_count == before_count
    finally:
        feature_flag_client.enable("onboarding-task")
